methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Canada Rocket Company is a seed-stage space and aerospace developer founded by Hugh Kolias and David Tenny. The company has raised $6.2M to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public Space & Aerospace peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private comparables including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Of three valuation methodologies considered, only stage-progression proved applicable. This method uses the company's $6.2M total raised as a proxy for seed-stage value and applies a 2.5x multiplier to estimate post-seed valuation, yielding $15.5M. Peer-median analysis was inapplicable due to insufficient narrow comparables in the seed-stage space sector—while 25 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three met tight filtering criteria on stage and valuation tier. Public-comp anchoring was also inapplicable; although the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S stands at 42.9x across the 5 public tickers, Canada Rocket Company does not disclose revenue, precluding this calculation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $18M, with a median point of $16M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodology. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x step-up multiplier for this specific sub-sector and lack of revenue or operational metrics to validate the stage-progression assumption. Additional disclosure of financial metrics or identification of closer seed-stage peers would strengthen conviction in this range.
